task_completion.description: The ReqView application is open and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ReqView application window is visible on the screen, displaying the Example Project.

task_completion.description: The SRS document is selected in the project navigation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left-hand navigation pane (Project pane), the 'SRS' document is highlighted or selected.

task_completion.description: The main workspace displays the requirements in a table format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The central document view shows a grid or table layout with column headers at the top, rather than a plain document or list view.

task_completion.description: The agent accesses the column configuration settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu (e.g., from right-clicking a column header), a column selection dialog, or a view configuration menu is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The 'Priority' column is visible in the SRS requirements table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column header explicitly labeled 'Priority' is visible in the main table view of the SRS document.

task_completion.description: The 'Verification' column is visible in the SRS requirements table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column header explicitly labeled 'Verification' (or 'Ver. Method') is visible in the main table view of the SRS document.

integrity.description: The agent used the ReqView GUI to configure the columns.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory screenshots show mouse clicks and menu interactions within the ReqView application to add the columns.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual ReqView application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual interface matches the ReqView application, not a mock-up, terminal, or generic text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by manually editing project files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows or text editors open showing manual edits to 'project.json' or 'SRS.json' to alter the view state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target document is 'SRS' within the ReqView Example Project. The task requires making two specific columns visible in the table view: 'Priority' (an attribute) and 'Verification' (a traceability link, which may also appear as 'Ver. Method'). Success is determined by visually confirming these two column headers are present in the main SRS requirements table.
